data.title: Trump suggests taking away licenses of TV stations that criticize him
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to cancel licenses of local broadcast networks if their content is critical of him.

Trump's remarks on the networks came soon after ABC (NYSE:DIS) suspended late-night host Jimmy Kimmel’s show indefinitely over his remarks on the death of Charlie Kirk, a pro-Trump political activist.

"That’s something that should be talked about for licensing, too. When you have a network and you have evening shows, and all they do is hit Trump,” Trump said while speaking to reporters on Air Force One. "They’re licensed. They’re not allowed to do that. They’re an arm of the Democrat party.”

"I read someplace that the networks were 97% against me again; I get 97% negative. And yet I won it easily. I won all seven swing states, the popular vote, whatever. They’re 97% against. They give me totally bad publicity, the press," Trump told reporters. 

"I would think maybe their license should be taken away. It will be up to Brendan Carr. I think Brendan Carr is outstanding. He’s a patriot. He loves our country, and he’s a tough guy. So we’ll have to see," Trump said.

Earlier in the day, FCC Chair Carr backed ABC's decision to pull Kimmel's show off the air and suggested that his regulatory agency could take action against TV broadcasters and said the networks have an "obligation to operate in the public interest."

In June, CBS said it will completely retire The Late Show after its host, Stephen Colbert, blasted Paramount’s $16M settlement with Trump over a Kamala Harris interview, calling it "a big fat bribe."

Earlier this week, Trump filed a $15B defamation and libel lawsuit against The New York Times (NYT), accusing the paper of serving as a “virtual mouthpiece” for the Democrats.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Hess Midstream trims growth plans as Chevron cuts Bakken rigs
data.description:
Hess Midstream (NYSE:HESM) -1.9% post-market Thursday after providing updated guidance for the rest of FY 2025 based on updated expectations, and for 2026-27 based on an expected decrease in Bakken rig activity by Chevron (CVX) from four to three drilling rigs beginning in Q4 2025.

Hess Midstream (NYSE:HESM) said it continues to expect long-term growth in gas throughput volumes through at least 2027 in the Bakken while oil throughput volumes are seen plateauing in 2026 due to lower planned rig activity.

The company also said it now expects relatively flat adjusted EBITDA in 2026 compared to 2025, while reducing FY 2025 gas throughput guidance to 455M-465M cf/day with gas processing volumes of 440M-450M cf/day, based on adverse weather conditions and maintenance in Q3 and lower expected third-party volumes in Q4.

Q3 2025 net income and adjusted EBITDA is expected at the lower end of previously announced guidance and FY 2025 net income and adjusted EBITDA is anticipated at the lower half of the prior guidance range, the company said.

data.title: RENT files to sell subscription rights for Class A common stock
data.description:
• Rent the Runway (NASDAQ:RENT) has filed to sell subscription rights to purchase up to 3,063,725 shares of Class A common stock at $4.08 per share.
  • This preliminary prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • Filing.

data.title: Ferguson prices $750 million of senior unsecured notes
data.description:
• Ferguson Enterprises (NYSE:FERG) has priced a public offering of $750 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.350% senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Weatherford International announces expansion of credit facility to $1 billion
data.description:
• Weatherford International (NASDAQ:WFRD) announced on Thursday it has increased its credit facility by $280 million with aggregate commitments of $1 billion, effective today.
  • The facility is now comprised of a $600 million revolver tranche, $400 million allocated to performance letters of credit, an increased accordion feature, which could expand lender commitments to up to $1.15 billion, subject to certain conditions, and extends maturity from 2028 to 2030.
  • As a result of this expansion, the company’s pro forma liquidity as of June 2025 now stands at $1.5 billion, further enhancing its financial flexibility and supporting long-term strategic initiatives.
  • WFRD -1.8% after hours to $64.31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FedEx in charts: Total international export ADV declines YY
Incyte gets FDA nod for Opzelura cream in atopic dermatitis treatment
data.description:
• Incyte (NASDAQ:INCY) announced that the U.S. FDA has approved Opzelura (ruxolitinib) cream 1.5%, for the chronic treatment of mild to moderate atopic dermatitis in non-immunocompromised children two years of age and older.
  • Opzelura (ruxolitinib) cream is already approved for the topical treatment of nonsegmental vitiligo in patients 12 years of age and older.

data.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup an attractive commercial opportunity for Nike - RBC Capital
data.description:
With the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaching and Nike (NYSE:NKE) making improvements in its running portfolio, the sportswear giant is in a solid position to see a steeper revenue recovery which could lead to a beat and raise cycle with limited downside pressures, leading RBC Capital to upgrade Nike (NYSE:NKE) to Outperform from Sector Perform along with an 18% boost to its price target, now at $90.

With a sizable marketing budget of nearly $5B in FY26 -- almost double its closest rival adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) (OTCQX:ADDDF) – RBC Capital analysts led by Piral Dadhania expects to see accelerating brand visibility.

This will be amplified by the increased commercial opportunity at the 2026 World Cup versus the 2022 event in Qatar, which took place right after the pandemic, and which occurred in mid-season for many country leagues.

While the qualification process for the tournament is ongoing, RBC’s Dadhania expects Nike (NYSE:NKE) will sponsor 6 of the top 10 FIFA-ranked international teams (France, England, Brazil, Portugal, Netherlands, and Croatia) while adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) (OTCQX:ADDDF) will likely only sponsor 4 teams (Argentina, Spain, Belgium, and Germany).

Using a Commercial Relevance index, RBC estimates the commercial opportunity for Nike during the World Cup is worth $1.3B split largely between FY26 (70%) and early FY27 (30%).

“This should provide a timely boost to Nike’s revenue growth profile at an opportune moment in Nike’s turnaround, offering the potential for ‘overshooting’ revenue growth profile,” Dadhania and team said in their note to clients, adding that Nike (NKE) is expected to use the World Cup to launch new products.

And elevated brand visibility should be beneficial particularly as Nike’s (NKE) product pipeline is already looking stronger, Danhania and team say.

“We expect improvements in its apparel line-up from early calendar 2026 as well as World Cup 2026 kit launches from March 2026 onwards.”

RBC Capital is the second firm in a little over a week to upgrade Nike (NKE) with TD Cowen  moving to a Buy from Hold last Wednesday.

Nike (NKE) will report fiscal first quarter results on September 30 with per share profit expected to decline from a year ago to $0.27 and revenue to decline another 15% to $10.96B. Compared to the prior quarter, however, EPS is expected to nearly double as CEO Elliott Hill’s turnaround efforts gain traction.

data.title: Genworth Financial announces up to $350M stock buyback program
data.description:
Genworth Financial (NYSE:GNW) announced on Thursday a new share repurchase program to buy back up to $350M of outstanding common stock.

Shares were -0.46% post market to $8.66.

The buybacks may be made at the company’s discretion from time to time in open market transactions, privately negotiated transactions, or other means.

The new authorization by the board is in addition to the company's $700M stock buyback program, of which $16M remains as of September 17.

Repurchases under the authorized program are set to be funded from the holding company cash.

data.title: Gevo signs deal with Biorecro to accelerate multi-year carbon credit offtake
data.description:
• Gevo (NASDAQ:GEVO) on Thursday said its subsidiary, Net-Zero Richardton has signed a Carbon Dioxide Removal Sales Agreement with Biorecro North America for the commercialization of carbon dioxide removal credits generated at the Gevo North Dakota facility.
  • This agreement is for voluntary credits and is expected to generate about $26 million in revenues for Gevo over five years with the option to expand the volumes under the transaction, the company said.
  • GEVO -2.87% after hours to $2.03.
  • Source: Press Release
